title: Eastern Religion in Eva
wikitext:
[]: Eastern Religion in Eva?             Quote Post by Lance of LoL » Tue Feb 05, 2013 6:38 pm Already ran a search and didn't find anything similar, so here goes, anyone notice any Eastern Religion and Symbolism in Eva? The one that comes to mind for me is when 01 is rejecting the Dummy Plug Gendo says to start over from 108. 108 having significance in Buddhism among others as being the number of vices a human has to overcome to reach enlightenment. Now other than that I can't pick up any more, but my observational skills are somewhat lacking so I'm wondering if there's any underline of these principles alongside the western symbolism.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Tue Feb 05, 2013 7:07 pm From the Instrumentality topic , by This Good Sir : SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Lance of LoL » Tue Feb 05, 2013 8:13 pm That's a very interesting perspective. I can see the parallels, though I believe he means Izanagi, not Izagami. Taking out the matter of the character perspectives, that's pretty cool, I didn't even realise a lot of that. Though it creates certain character parallels between the generally passionate Kaworu and the distant Rei more than Asuka in my in my opinion

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Feb 05, 2013 8:20 pm There are plenty of 108's all across the frachise. They just generally love unlucky numbers. While the LoL takes its name from Christian mythology, I've seen some ppl on a German fansite that may or may not still exist suggest that it's actually a reference to Izanagi's spear. And little shrines make an appearance in episodes 3 and 4, more as BG thingies, though. For what it's worth, the CR says that the concept of a "Miko (shintoistic shrine maiden) channeling the gods" partiallly influenced Mari's creation, though it was more the idea of "Normal human girl who is connected/knowledgeable about the supernatural elements of the setting and calls forth gods (EVAs)" than any actual shintoism, not that I have that much of an idea about it. After all, Mari herself may or may not have gone to a catholic school... There was also someone of the German EVA wiki suggesting that Kaworu, the last angel, looking like a human may be an allusion to how Ying and Yang are supposed to form mirror images just before they reenter their undivided state (instrumentality) Looking at the cast themselves, Misato seems to fold her hands in prayer in episode 21 (just before she notices that Kaji left her a message. - could just be a generic "Please let him be alive!" reaction or simply a pose meaning to indicate the onset of grief, though. ), Rei seems unsure/ appears to have been wondering wether God or Gods exists and created humans in episode 14. Judging from his reaction to the idea of his mother watching him from the heavens (ep 15), at least Shinji appears to be a non-believer. (Which is fairly common in Japan, as far as I know) We have no real indications with the rest of the cast... There are general statements on "Gods" here and there, especially in all of the instrumentality babble, but they probably mean the FAR half the time and the simple philosophic concept of a "God" the other half of the time, invoking it to characterize the human condition.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Tue Feb 05, 2013 8:33 pm Kaworu's just an inexplicable suicide bitch. He wants to die, much like Rei does, though his motivations may be slightly different. And the parallels between Rei and Asuka in Instrumentality are entirely clear, Rei and Asuka state their opposite goals in succession. And yes, Izanagi is correct, I'll go change that. Oh Kendrix, thank you! I knew Adam and Lilith were certainly Izanagi and Izanami! I should have remembered they had a spear!

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Wed Feb 06, 2013 9:11 am The ultimate goal of Buddhism, from what I understand, is to completely lose your sense of "self" so you can no longer suffer in the world and obtain Nirvana, because all life is suffering, and happiness is only a fleeting, momentary thing. Reminds me a lot of Instrumentality.

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Wed Feb 06, 2013 3:06 pm Lavinius wrote: Kaworu's just an inexplicable suicide bitch. He wants to die, much like Rei does, though his motivations may be slightly different. And the parallels between Rei and Asuka in Instrumentality are entirely clear, Rei and Asuka state their opposite goals in succession. And yes, Izanagi is correct, I'll go change that. Oh Kendrix, thank you! I knew Adam and Lilith were certainly Izanagi and Izanami! I should have remembered they had a spear!

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Wed Feb 06, 2013 4:21 pm Well, Asuka screams that she doesn't want to be alone, which is fairly self-explanatory. There is also the more general explanation that she wants to not suffer and to be entirely happy forever. In the elevator scene, Rei says that she would do anything for Commander Ikari, and confirms that she would kill herself if he asked her to. Asuka, a westerner, is horrified. But to be utterly obedient to one's master to the point of suicide was of course historically considered to be the height of virtue in Japan.

[]:             Quote Post by Lance of LoL » Wed Feb 06, 2013 4:27 pm I think we're drifting more into the character's theological positions here as opposed to the actual symbolism of eastern religious culture in Eva. I'm sure more blatant things like the shrines and the style of the graves are gonna crop up since the series is set in Japan, but I'm trying to find the more subtle references. Though on that note, the spear used by Izanagi and Izanami was the Ama no Hoko, literally "Spear of Heaven" which was used to churn the oceans and create the islands of Japan. I think there's a parallel to be made here in that the Kisaragi expedition turned the spear on Adam and basically bollocks the oceans for all to see.
